sub_test: Aggregate failure: test idempotency of endpoints (9/9 scenario checks failed)
title: [test-idempotency-of-endpoints] Aggregate failure: test idempotency of endpoints (9/9 scenario checks failed) on n/a — aggregate finding across endpoints — expected All 9 'test idempotency of endpoints' scenario checks pass against the documented behaviour, observed [api-tester-test-idempotency-of-endpoints] quality outcome=FAIL; metric=0.0 (undocumented)
expected: All 9 'test idempotency of endpoints' scenario checks pass against the documented behaviour

testing_steps: 1. Start the target API at http://localhost:8899.
2. Write a script that sends the SAME PUT http://localhost:8899/products/1 payload twice and the same DELETE /products/1 twice, capturing status+body each time.
3. Assert each replay returns an identical outcome with no duplicated side effects, and that a repeated POST /products/add behaves per the documented create semantics.
4. Compare your script's failures with the per-scenario evidence in TestCases/test-idempotency-of-endpoints/cases.json — this run recorded 9 of 9 checks failing.

description: The test-idempotency-of-endpoints check failed in aggregate against http://localhost:8899: [api-tester-test-idempotency-of-endpoints] quality outcome=FAIL; metric=0.0. The per-scenario evidence is in TestCases/test-idempotency-of-endpoints/cases.json. There is no single reproducible API call — reproduce it with the script described in the steps below (which is also why this bug has no Postman section).
steps_to_reproduce: 1. Start the target API at http://localhost:8899.
2. Write a script that sends the SAME PUT http://localhost:8899/products/1 payload twice and the same DELETE /products/1 twice, capturing status+body each time.
3. Assert each replay returns an identical outcome with no duplicated side effects, and that a repeated POST /products/add behaves per the documented create semantics.
4. Compare your script's failures with the per-scenario evidence in TestCases/test-idempotency-of-endpoints/cases.json — this run recorded 9 of 9 checks failing.
